Here's what I found before I touch anything — this needs a decision from you.

**Branch comparison:**
- `feat/word-spell-library-levels-recall` — **up to date**. Has all 18 BKP commits (rebased), plus 2 newer fixes (`drop emoji rounds…`, `route resolver honours simple-mode…`), plus the entire `design/kid-friendly-homepage` stack underneath.
- `feat/word-spell-library-levels-recall__BKP` — older snapshot. Every commit on it has a counterpart on `recall` with the same message but rebased SHA. Nothing unique.

**Master moved on:**
PR #108 (`feat(homepage): kid-friendly homepage + simple config`) merged the kid-friendly-homepage stack into master, plus 3 a11y fixes. So `recall` and `master` overlap heavily on homepage commits, and a plain `git rebase master` from `recall` will conflict messily on ~90 files (recall is 2288 ins / 8635 del vs BKP just for context).

**Worktree note:**
The on-disk worktree at `worktrees/feat-word-spell-library-levels-recall` is checked out on `__BKP`, not `recall`. I haven't modified anything.

**Recommended path (please confirm before I proceed):**
1. Delete `__BKP` locally + remote (it has no unique work).
2. Switch the worktree to `recall`.
3. Rebase `recall` onto `origin/master` — expect conflicts where homepage commits collide with #108. Resolve by dropping the already-merged homepage commits and keeping only the word-spell-library-levels work.
4. Then run the full pipeline locally: typecheck, lint, unit, storybook, build, e2e, VR (Docker required).
